Create support-safe diagnostics bundles

Usage:
  laurel diagnostics [--config <path>] [--output <file>] [--log-lines <n>] [--dry-run] [--list] [--json] <subcommand>

Arguments:
  subcommand          `bundle` (write a redacted diagnostics .tar.gz)

End of options:
  Use `--` before positional values that start with `-`, for example `laurel new post -- --config`.

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  -o, --output <file> Path for the .tar.gz bundle. Defaults to laurel-diagnostics-<timestamp>.tar.gz in the current directory
  --log-lines <n>     Maximum number of lines to include from each known Laurel log file. Defaults to 200; use 0 to omit log text while still listing log candidates
  --dry-run           Print the archive path and entry list without writing a bundle. Useful for auditing what support artifacts would be collected
  --list              Alias for --dry-run: list planned bundle entries without writing the archive
  -j, --json          Emit the bundle result as JSON ({ output, entries, bytes, dryRun }) for CI or support scripts
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → LAUREL_DIAGNOSTICS_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel diagnostics bundle
  laurel diagnostics bundle --output support/laurel-diagnostics.tar.gz
  laurel diagnostics bundle --dry-run
  laurel diagnostics bundle --log-lines 50 --json
